title: Tangy Crispy Peruvian Lamb Secco de Cordero
description: A midnight-craving Peruvian dish featuring crispy lamb, spring onions, and a tangy marinade. Paleo-friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine lime juice, garlic, aji amarillo peppers, olive oil, salt, and black pepper. Blend until smooth.
In a large bowl, whisk together the marinade and 2 tablespoons of water. Add the lamb shoulder and coat evenly.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight, flipping the lamb halfway through.
Remove the lamb from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Place the lamb on a wire rack set over a rimmed baking sheet or a broiler pan.
Roast in the preheated oven for 20 minutes per pound, or until the lamb reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
During the last 10 minutes of cooking, increase the oven temperature to broil (high).
While the lamb is cooking, thinly slice the spring onions and separate them into rings.
After the lamb has finished cooking, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the lamb against the grain into thin strips.
To serve, place a portion of the lamb on a plate and top with a spoonful of sliced spring onions and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.
Garnish with additional spring onions and cilantro, if desired.
